resposta:
```python
class Solution:
    def lexicalOrder(self, n: int) -> List[int]:
        result = []
        i = 1
        while i <= n:
            result.append(i)
            i += 1
        return result
```
solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def lexicalOrder(self, n: int) -> List[int]:
        result = []
        i = 1
        while i <= n:
            result.append(i)
            i += 1
        return result
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation:  Generates a list of numbers from 1 to n and appends them to the result list.  This approach is straightforward and has a time complexity of O(n) as it iterates through the numbers once. The space complexity is also O(n) due to the size of the result list.
